actionable_feedback: Add explicit remediation cost accounting to the follow-up memo and/or a companion cost table: for each remediation option (O1/O2/O3) provide unit cost, estimated quantity or application scope (per-record or per-action), aggregated cost per record and aggregated cost for the recommended set. Show the total cost and demonstrate it is <= remediation_budget_usd (25000).

State a smallest-sufficient recommendation: identify which records will receive remediation, which specific option(s) will be used for each record (O1/O2/O3), and justify the choice with a short cost/benefit note. Explicitly show that the chosen set is the minimal set meeting the remediation objective and fits the budget.

Add cost fields to methane_plume_filter.csv (or attach a small CSV/table) with per-record cost calculations and a final aggregation row so that automated checks can verify total cost <= remediation_budget_usd.

Ensure the full field_followup.md content is visible/non-truncated when re-submitting so graders can verify the cost accounting. If file-size or formatting caused truncation, split long appendices into separate artifacts and reference them.

Run and include deterministic recomputation checks (brief section) confirming derived numeric fields in the CSV match the pseudo-formulas in the memo; include a checksum or sample recomputation lines to help verifiers validate automatically.

Fix any internal metadata/serialization inconsistencies before re-submission (avoid duplicated/conflicting 'passed' or 'final_score' fields in grader outputs) so automated verifiers and graders can unambiguously read the status.

feedback_summary: Required artifacts were produced and contain traceable derivations and row-level citations. However, the deliverable is missing explicit remediation cost accounting and a smallest-sufficient remediation recommendation with aggregated costs constrained to remediation_budget_usd. The field_followup.md preview is truncated in the grader/verifier view; the grader flagged (and the verifier confirmed) the absence of remediation cost lines in the available previews. Also, the grader/verifier payloads show some internal consistency/representation issues (duplicated/contradictory pass/score fields) that should be cleaned for unambiguous grading.

recommended_next_steps_for_reviewer: Re-evaluate the grade after full field_followup.md is made available (no truncation).
Verify added cost-accounting lines in the memo and presence of cost columns/aggregation in CSV.
Run deterministic recomputation on the final CSV to confirm all derived numeric fields match documented formulas.
